relevant_files.role: EXTENSION_MAP with cfg gates; language_for_extension() lookup function; supported_languages() array
LanguageInfo struct (14 fields); get_language_info() exhaustive match; get_ts_language() getter; module declarations with cfg gates
Reference implementation: ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, DEFUSE_QUERY constants; extract_inheritance handler
Reference implementation: find_method_for_receiver handler; extract_inheritance for embedded types
Reference implementation: DEFUSE_QUERY pattern for write/read sites; extract_inheritance for superclasses
Feature flags section; optional dependencies; default features array
Integration test patterns for language support

conventions.commits: Conventional commits with format 'feat(lang): add X grammar support (#NNN)'; GPG signed with DCO sign-off; all commits in feature branch via PR
conventions.testing: Unit tests in language module under #[cfg(all(test, feature = 'lang-X'))]; parse_X() helper function to create Parser, set language, parse source; integration tests in crates/code-analyze-core/tests/; semantic correctness tests in semantic_correctness.rs; each language has 8-12 unit tests covering happy path and edge cases
conventions.linting: cargo clippy -- -D warnings (mandatory); cargo fmt --check (mandatory); cargo deny check advisories licenses (mandatory); all must pass before merge
conventions.error_handling: thiserror crate for error types; Result<T> return types; expect() with descriptive messages in tests; no unwrap() in production code paths
conventions.imports: tree_sitter::* for parsing APIs; use super::* in test modules; conditional module loading via #[cfg(feature = 'lang-X')]; workspace dependencies for tree-sitter-* crates

patterns: ELEMENT_QUERY: captures function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration with @function/@class labels; uses named children (name field) for symbol extraction
CALL_QUERY: captures call_expression nodes with @call label; models both direct calls and method invocations
REFERENCE_QUERY: captures type identifiers in type contexts (simple_identifier in type annotations, user_type in delegation specifiers); optional for some languages
IMPORT_QUERY: captures import_header nodes; covers all import forms (namespace, static, aliased)
DEFUSE_QUERY: captures write sites (assignment, update, declaration) and read sites (usage); uses @write.X and @read.X labels; optional but recommended
extract_inheritance: walks delegation_specifiers or equivalent to collect superclass and interface names; returns Vec<String> with formatted names (e.g., 'extends Foo', 'implements Bar')
find_method_for_receiver: optional handler; returns method name for a given receiver type; used for method dispatch analysis
extract_function_name: optional handler; extracts function name from declaration node; not all languages implement

Unit test pattern: parse_X() helper creates Parser, sets language, parses source; test_X_element_query_happy_path verifies query compilation and node matching; test_X_extract_inheritance verifies inheritance extraction; test_X_defuse_query_write_site verifies def-use sites

Feature gates: applied to module declaration (mod kotlin;), EXTENSION_MAP entries (.kt, .kts), supported_languages() array entry, get_language_info() match arm, get_ts_language() match arm, all unit tests

related_issues.relevance: Primary issue; defines acceptance criteria, implementation notes, node kinds, and delegation specifiers syntax

related_issues.title: chore: drop unused language grammars (kotlin, swift, ruby, javascript)
related_issues.relevance: Closed via PR #63 (merged 2026-03-03); removed Kotlin from codebase; this issue re-adds it with proper integration

constraints: Kotlin delegation_specifiers syntax: class Foo : Bar(), Baz { } where Bar() is superclass (constructor call with parens), Baz is interface (no parens); extract_inheritance must distinguish these via node inspection
No separate impl blocks in Kotlin (unlike Rust); inheritance is inline in class declaration; impl_query and impl_trait_query must be None
Companion objects are nested inside class body; declared as 'companion object { ... }'; no special extraction needed for symbol analysis
tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 is pinned version per issue requirement; must be added to workspace.dependencies in root Cargo.toml

All language handlers must implement LanguageInfo struct contract: 14 fields (name, language, element_query, call_query, reference_query, import_query, impl_query, impl_trait_query, defuse_query, extract_function_name, find_method_for_receiver, find_receiver_type, extract_inheritance); some optional (None allowed)
DEFUSE_QUERY is optional but recommended for semantic analysis; most languages implement it (Rust, Python, Go, Java, TypeScript, JavaScript, C/C++, C#); Fortran omits it
All feature gates must be consistent across: Cargo.toml feature definition, module declaration, EXTENSION_MAP, supported_languages(), get_language_info(), get_ts_language(), unit tests
lang-kotlin must be added to default features in crates/code-analyze-core/Cargo.toml; all languages are enabled by default
test_coverage: Existing test infrastructure: test_language_for_extension_happy_path and test_language_for_extension_edge_case in lang.rs verify extension registration; test_get_language_info_known/unknown and test_get_ts_language_known/unknown in languages/mod.rs verify language info retrieval. Per-language unit tests in each language module (8-12 tests per language) covering: element query compilation and node matching (happy path), edge cases (empty code, malformed syntax), inheritance extraction (single/multiple/mixed), def-use sites (write/read/update), method dispatch. Integration tests in crates/code-analyze-core/tests/integration_tests.rs and semantic_correctness.rs verify end-to-end analysis. New Kotlin tests must follow same pattern: unit tests in kotlin.rs (test_kotlin_element_query_happy_path, test_kotlin_extract_inheritance, test_kotlin_defuse_query_write_site, etc.), integration tests in integration_tests.rs, semantic correctness test in semantic_correctness.rs. Minimum 10 unit tests required; 3 integration tests recommended.

library_findings.notes: Core parsing library; used by all language handlers. APIs: Parser::new(), Parser::set_language(), Parser::parse(), Query::new(), QueryCursor::matches(), Node::child_by_field_name(), Node::named_child(), Node::kind(), Node::start_byte(), Node::end_byte()

library_findings.relevant_api: tree_sitter_kotlin::LANGUAGE; node kinds: function_declaration, class_declaration, interface_declaration, enum_class_declaration, object_declaration, call_expression, simple_identifier, import_header, delegation_specifiers
library_findings.notes: Kotlin grammar for tree-sitter (fwcd/tree-sitter-kotlin). Key node kinds: function_declaration (free functions), class_declaration (classes, data classes via modifiers), interface_declaration (interfaces), enum_class_declaration (enums), object_declaration (objects, companion objects via modifiers), call_expression (calls), simple_identifier (identifiers), import_header (imports), delegation_specifiers (inheritance with superclass_type_with_constructor for superclass with parens, user_type for interfaces without parens)

approaches.name: Approach 1: Minimal Implementation (No DEFUSE_QUERY)
approaches.description: Add tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 to workspace dependencies and create kotlin.rs with ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, and extract_inheritance handler. Omit DEFUSE_QUERY to minimize scope. Register .kt and .kts extensions in lang.rs, add lang-kotlin feature flag in Cargo.toml, register kotlin in get_language_info() and get_ts_language() in languages/mod.rs. Include 8-10 unit tests (element query, call query, inheritance extraction with superclass and interfaces, edge cases). No find_method_for_receiver or extract_function_name handlers.
pros: Smallest implementation scope; reduces risk of bugs
Meets all 12 acceptance criteria from issue
DEFUSE_QUERY can be added in follow-up PR if needed
Aligns with Fortran approach (which also omits DEFUSE_QUERY)
cons: No def-use site extraction; limits semantic analysis capabilities
Inconsistent with 8 of 9 other languages (all have DEFUSE_QUERY except Fortran)
May require follow-up work to add DEFUSE_QUERY later
Reduces value for users relying on write/read site detection

approaches.name: Approach 2: Complete Implementation (With DEFUSE_QUERY)
approaches.description: Add tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 to workspace dependencies and create kotlin.rs with ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, DEFUSE_QUERY, and extract_inheritance handler. DEFUSE_QUERY captures local variable declarations, assignments, updates, and identifier usage (write/read sites). Register .kt and .kts extensions in lang.rs, add lang-kotlin feature flag in Cargo.toml, register kotlin in get_language_info() and get_ts_language() in languages/mod.rs. Include 12-14 unit tests covering all query types and def-use site extraction. No find_method_for_receiver or extract_function_name handlers.
pros: Consistent with 8 of 9 other languages (all except Fortran)
Enables semantic analysis for write/read site detection
No follow-up work needed; feature-complete on first PR
Better value for users relying on def-use analysis
cons: Larger implementation scope; more test cases to write
Requires careful tuning of DEFUSE_QUERY patterns for Kotlin syntax
More potential for edge case bugs in def-use extraction

approaches.name: Approach 3: Extended Implementation (With DEFUSE_QUERY + find_method_for_receiver)
approaches.description: Add tree-sitter-kotlin 0.3.8 to workspace dependencies and create kotlin.rs with ELEMENT_QUERY, CALL_QUERY, REFERENCE_QUERY, IMPORT_QUERY, DEFUSE_QUERY, extract_inheritance, and find_method_for_receiver handlers. find_method_for_receiver walks parent chain from a method node to find enclosing class/interface and returns method name. Register .kt and .kts extensions in lang.rs, add lang-kotlin feature flag in Cargo.toml, register kotlin in get_language_info() and get_ts_language() in languages/mod.rs. Include 14-16 unit tests covering all query types, def-use sites, and method dispatch. Justification: Kotlin has rich method dispatch semantics (extension functions, receiver types, operator overloading); find_method_for_receiver enables precise call graph analysis.
pros: Most comprehensive implementation; enables advanced call graph analysis
Supports Kotlin-specific features (extension functions, receiver types)
Consistent with Rust, Go, C++ approaches (which implement find_method_for_receiver)
Future-proofs against feature requests for method dispatch
Best value for users analyzing complex Kotlin codebases
cons: Requires deep understanding of Kotlin method dispatch semantics
Most test cases to write and maintain
Longest review cycle; more potential for edge case bugs
May be over-engineered if find_method_for_receiver is not used by MCP tools

recommendation: Approach 2: Complete Implementation (With DEFUSE_QUERY). Rationale: Meets all 12 acceptance criteria from issue #649; consistent with 8 of 9 other languages; enables semantic analysis for write/read site detection without over-engineering. DEFUSE_QUERY is straightforward for Kotlin (similar to Java) and adds significant value for users. find_method_for_receiver (Approach 3) is optional per issue spec and can be deferred to a follow-up PR if call graph analysis becomes a priority. Approach 1 (minimal) leaves a feature gap that will likely require follow-up work. Approach 2 balances completeness, consistency, and implementation effort.
